items.title: Claude Fable won’t answer basic biology questions
items.raw_content: Anthropic just released Claude Fable 5, calling it the most powerful AI model it has ever made widely available and praising its skills in biology, among others. But the model won't answer basic biology questions - the kind you'd expect a high schooler to handle. Instead, it hands off the query to the former flagship […]

items.title: Microsoft restricts Claude Fable for employees over data retention concerns
items.raw_content: Anthropic released Claude Fable, its first Mythos-class AI model, yesterday and it's already causing concerns inside Microsoft. Sources tell me that Microsoft is limiting the use of Claude Fable 5 for employees because of Anthropic's new data retention requirements. While Microsoft quickly rolled out Claude Fable 5 to its GitHub Copilot and Foundry customers, I'm […]

items.title: Datadog veterans launch AI coding startup Niteshift on a bet against Big AI lock-in
items.raw_content: AI coding agent startup Niteshift has raised a $7 million seed round from a who's who of angels. It's betting companies will want power over, not lock-in with model makers.
items.summary: AI coding agent startup Niteshift has raised a $7 million seed round from a who's who of angels. It's betting companies will want power over, not lock-in with model makers.

items.title: Anthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable
items.raw_content: Anthropic just announced Claude Fable 5, a new AI model it said is the most powerful model it has ever made widely available. According to the company, Fable 5 "shows exceptional performance in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision," with its lead over other models growing as tasks become longer and more complex. Fable 5 […]

items.title: Jedify raises $24M to help companies arm AI agents with context on their business
items.raw_content: The funding round was led by Norwest, with participation from S Capital VC, Cerca Partners, and Oceans Ventures. Snowflake Ventures also participated as a strategic investor.
items.summary: The funding round was led by Norwest, with participation from S Capital VC, Cerca Partners, and Oceans Ventures. Snowflake Ventures also participated as a strategic investor.

items.title: xAI fired an engineer who raised alarms about Grok safety, new lawsuit claims
items.raw_content: A former xAI engineer is suing the company and SpaceX, alleging he was fired for raising AI safety concerns about Grok days before SpaceX's historic IPO.
items.summary: A former xAI engineer is suing the company and SpaceX, alleging he was fired for raising AI safety concerns about Grok days before SpaceX's historic IPO.

items.title: Decart’s new world model can simulate hours of photorealistic driving — with some caveats
items.raw_content: Decart is launching Oasis 3, a real-time world model that generates photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing, now available via API for developers to build on.
items.summary: Decart is launching Oasis 3, a real-time world model that generates photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing, now available via API for developers to build on.

items.title: Cybersecurity researchers aren’t happy about the guardrails on Anthropic’s Fable
items.raw_content: Cybersecurity researchers are complaining that Anthropic's new model Fable has guardrails that are too strict for any cybersecurity work.
items.summary: Cybersecurity researchers are complaining that Anthropic's new model Fable has guardrails that are too strict for any cybersecurity work.

items.title: Fresh off bond sale, Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending continues
items.raw_content: Companies are burning through exorbitant sums of money to keep pace in the AI arms race. Debt is climbing.
items.summary: Companies are burning through exorbitant sums of money to keep pace in the AI arms race. Debt is climbing.

items.title: ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
items.raw_content: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.
items.summary: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

items.title: How memory tools can make AI models worse
items.raw_content: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.
items.summary: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.

items.title: Warner Music acquires AI attribution startup Sureel AI
items.raw_content: Through the acquisition, WMG aims to better track when its artists' work is used in AI-generated content or for training AI models.
items.summary: Through the acquisition, WMG aims to better track when its artists' work is used in AI-generated content or for training AI models.

items.title: Microsoft, like, totally gets why students are booing AI-pilled graduation speakers
items.raw_content: New college graduates around the country have been booing and heckling commencement speakers who hype up AI. Microsoft would like everyone to talk it out. In a blog post running more than 3,100 words, Microsoft vice chair and president Brad Smith addressed the recent spate of viral clips from graduation ceremonies, like former Google CEO […]

items.title: Google won’t just admit it’s feeding YouTube creators to its music AI
items.raw_content: If you've uploaded a song to YouTube, Google almost certainly considers your video fair game for training its Lyria music AI, it just won't admit it right now. A group of independent musicians is suing Google, claiming that it illegally used songs they uploaded to YouTube to train its Lyria 3 model. Google has filed […]

items.title: Google will save your Lens photos, Search Live recordings, and Translate audio for AI training
items.raw_content: Google is making some changes to how it saves your interactions with Search. In an email sent to users, Google says it will save the images, files, audio, and video you use to search under a new "Search Services History" setting. That includes the images you search for with Google Lens, recordings from its real-time […]

items.title: The three hard-tech moonshots fueling SpaceX’s unbelievable IPO
items.raw_content: Most of the value in SpaceX's IPO is effectively a call option on the company's ambitious space data center plans.
items.summary: Most of the value in SpaceX's IPO is effectively a call option on the company's ambitious space data center plans.

items.title: Microsoft AI head calls out Anthropic for acting like Claude is conscious
items.raw_content: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman says it's "really, really dangerous" for Anthropic to speculate about Claude's consciousness inside its "constitution," or the instructions that tell the model how to behave. During an episode of Decoder, Suleyman argues that this kind of speculation may have set up the chatbot to act as though it's conscious: I […]

items.title: GM thinks EVs can help offset AI’s energy suck with vehicle-to-grid tech
items.raw_content: At an event in San Francisco today, General Motors made a series of announcements around EV batteries, energy storage, and grid resiliency in the face of growing electricity demand from AI data centers. items.title: I tried Siri AI, and so far it actually works
items.raw_content: Parents want one thing, and one thing only, out of AI: to add a list of soccer games or "spirit week" theme days from an email or a poorly formatted flyer onto their calendar in one shot. And I have good news for parents with iPhones - the new Siri can finally do this. After […]
